# Interface Contracts: Clay Idea Entrypoint

**Purpose**: Define the exact structure and behavior contracts for the skill files so implementation tasks produce compatible, spec-compliant output. Since clay skills are Markdown files (not code modules), contracts define file structure, section requirements, and behavioral contracts rather than function signatures.

**Generated by**: `/plan`
**Consumed by**: `/implement`

## Rules

- Every skill file MUST include the sections defined here
- Behavioral contracts (what the skill does at each step) MUST match these definitions
- If a contract needs to change during implementation, update this file FIRST
- clay.config format MUST match the exact specification below

## File: plugin-clay/skills/idea/SKILL.md (NEW)

### Frontmatter

```yaml
---
name: idea
description: Primary entrypoint for the clay plugin. Takes a raw idea, checks products/ and clay.config for overlap with existing products and repos, then routes to the right next step. Use as the starting point for any new idea.
---
```

### Required Sections (in order)

```markdown
# Idea — Clay Entrypoint

## User Input
# FR-001: Accept $ARGUMENTS as idea description (1-5 sentences)
# Prompt for input if $ARGUMENTS is empty

## Step 1: Gather Context
# FR-002: Read all products/<slug>/ directories
#   - For each: read research.md, naming.md, PRD.md (extract name, description, summary)
#   - Build a list of existing products with their key attributes
# FR-003, FR-010: Read clay.config (if it exists)
#   - Parse each line: <product-slug> <repo-url> <local-path> <created-date>
#   - Skip malformed lines with a warning
# FR-012: If clay.config doesn't exist, skip repo-based detection gracefully

## Step 2: Overlap Analysis
# FR-004: Compare input idea against:
#   - Existing products (from products/ scan)
#   - Tracked repos (from clay.config)
# Use semantic reasoning (LLM), not string matching
# For each match, explain WHY it overlaps

## Step 3: Present Routing Options
# FR-005: Present findings with these exact routing options:
#   - "New product" — no overlap found
#   - "Add to existing product" — overlap with product in products/
#   - "Work in existing repo" — overlap with tracked repo in clay.config
#   - "Similar but distinct" — partial overlap, let user decide
# Always show the reasoning behind the recommendation
# Always ask for user confirmation before proceeding

## Step 4: Route to Downstream Skill
# FR-006: After user confirms a route, chain to the appropriate skill
# NEVER auto-execute without user confirmation
# FR-007: For "New product" route:
#   1. Run /idea-research with the idea description
#   2. After research completes, offer /project-naming
#   3. After naming completes, offer /create-prd
#   Each step requires user confirmation before proceeding
```

### Behavioral Contracts

| Behavior | Contract |
|----------|----------|
| Empty input | MUST prompt user for 1-5 sentence idea description |
| No products/ dir | MUST skip product scan, proceed to clay.config check |
| No clay.config | MUST skip repo scan, proceed to analysis with products only |
| No overlap found | MUST recommend "New product" route |
| Product overlap | MUST name the matching product and explain why |
| Repo overlap | MUST show repo URL and local path from clay.config |
| Multiple overlaps | MUST list all matches with individual reasoning |
| User confirms route | MUST chain to correct downstream skill |
| "New product" chain | MUST follow: idea-research → project-naming → create-prd (sequential) |
| "Add to existing" chain | MUST invoke /create-prd in Mode C targeting matched product |
| "Work in existing repo" | MUST suggest cd + /build-prd or /clay:idea in that repo |

## File: plugin-clay/skills/create-repo/SKILL.md (MODIFIED)

### New Step: Write to clay.config (between existing Step 7 and Step 8)

```markdown
## Step 7.5: Update Clay Config Registry
# FR-009, FR-013: Append repo entry to clay.config
# Format: <product-slug> <repo-url> <local-path> <created-date>
# Use >> (append), never > (overwrite)
# Create clay.config if it doesn't exist
# Only write if repo creation succeeded (Step 7 completed)
```

### Bash Contract

```bash
# FR-009: Append to clay.config (sync — no async)
# Params: SLUG (string), REPO_URL (string), LOCAL_PATH (string), DATE (string YYYY-MM-DD)
# Side effect: creates or appends to clay.config at project root
echo "<SLUG> <REPO_URL> <LOCAL_PATH> <DATE>" >> clay.config
```

### Behavioral Contracts

| Behavior | Contract |
|----------|----------|
| clay.config doesn't exist | MUST create it with the new entry |
| clay.config exists | MUST append (not overwrite) |
| Repo creation failed | MUST NOT write to clay.config |
| Entry format | MUST be: `<slug> <url> <local-path> <YYYY-MM-DD>` |

## File: plugin-clay/skills/clay-list/SKILL.md (MODIFIED)

### Modified Step: Read clay.config

```markdown
## Step 1.5: Read Clay Config
# FR-011, FR-014: Read clay.config if it exists
# Parse entries and build a lookup map: slug → {url, local_path, date}
# FR-012: If clay.config doesn't exist, skip — no repo columns in output
```

### Modified Output Table

```markdown
# When clay.config EXISTS:
| Product        | Status       | Artifacts | Repo URL                              | Local Path     |
|----------------|--------------|-----------|---------------------------------------|----------------|
| my-product     | repo-created | 5/6       | https://github.com/user/my-product    | ../my-product  |
| another-idea   | researched   | 1/6       | —                                     | —              |

# When clay.config DOES NOT EXIST:
| Product        | Status       | Artifacts |
|----------------|--------------|-----------|
| my-product     | prd-created  | 4/6       |
| another-idea   | researched   | 1/6       |
```

### Behavioral Contracts

| Behavior | Contract |
|----------|----------|
| clay.config exists | MUST add Repo URL and Local Path columns |
| clay.config missing | MUST render table without repo columns (not empty columns) |
| Product has clay.config entry | MUST show URL and path from config |
| Product has no clay.config entry | MUST show "—" in repo columns |
| Malformed clay.config lines | MUST skip silently |

## Format: clay.config

```text
# Plain-text registry of repos created by /clay:create-repo
# Format: <product-slug> <repo-url> <local-path> <created-date>
# FR-008: One entry per line, space-separated fields
# Lines starting with # are comments

habit-tracker https://github.com/yoshisada/habit-tracker ../habit-tracker 2026-04-07
notes-app https://github.com/yoshisada/notes-app ../notes-app 2026-04-05
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| product-slug | string (kebab-case) | Product directory name under products/ |
| repo-url | string (URL) | GitHub repo URL |
| local-path | string (path) | Where the repo was cloned locally |
| created-date | string (YYYY-MM-DD) | Date the repo was created |
